# Changelog CSS-001: index.css Syntax & Compatibility Fixes

## Aim of the Change
The aim was to resolve a syntax error in the `body` selector and fix cross-browser compatibility issues related to CSS masks in the `index.css` file.

## What was Changed
1.  **Syntax Fix**: Corrected line 145 by changing `min-h-screen;` to `@apply min-h-screen;`. Direct usage of Tailwind utilities without `@apply` inside standard CSS blocks is a syntax error.
2.  **Cross-Browser Masks**: Added standard `mask` properties alongside specialized `-webkit-mask` properties in the `.glass::after` pseudo-element. This ensures that the refraction rim effect renders correctly in Firefox and other non-Chromium browsers.

## Result of the Change
- Eliminated IDE "colon expected" and browser support errors.
- Improved the robustness of the "Liquid Glass" effect across different browsers.

## Inspection & Course of Action
I inspected the file at the reported line numbers and identified that line 145 was missing the Tailwind `@apply` directive. I also confirmed that the `mask` property was using vendor-specific prefixes without a standard fallback, which triggers warnings.

## Tests Done
- Verified that the `npm run dev` process (already running in the background) does not show new CSS compilation errors.
- Visual inspection of the "Liquid Glass" elements confirms that the rim refraction remains visible.

## Overall Understanding
Maintaining clean CSS is critical for performance and cross-browser stability. This change ensures that the high-fidelity design system implemented in [LG-001](LG-001.md) adheres to web standards.
